prompt:
Create a striking vertical 9:16 cinematic product-poster for NuLumin Bio-Sciences 'Endocrine Research'. Aesthetic: vivid, bright, eye-catching luxury pharma editorial — saturated and bold, NOT dark or moody. Inspired by the booth scatter shots (bright color background with floating vials) but elevated.

THREE PEPTIDE VIALS (HEROES): KissPeptin 10mg (REFERENCE 1), Ipamorelin 10mg (REFERENCE 2), PT-141 5mg (REFERENCE 3) — each with its signature pink cap and the EXACT NuLumin label artwork preserved (italic NuLumin wordmark on top, 'BIO-SCIENCES' subtext, italic peptide name, bold pink mg dose, 'Manufactured by NuLumin' line, small side info block). Do NOT alter any label text.

VIAL POSE — DYNAMIC FALLING: the three vials look as if they have been gently tossed into the air and are caught mid-fall by the camera. Each vial is at a different rotation and angle (one tumbling forward, one nearly vertical, one tilted backward), suspended at eye-level / center of the canvas. Subtle implied motion — slight gravitational descent feel without aggressive motion blur (labels stay sharp and readable). Vials are SMALL — roughly 22–28% of canvas height each — clustered loosely in the middle third with generous space at top and bottom and ample side margins. The composition feels alive and weightless, premium and editorial.

BACKGROUND — BRIGHT AND THEMATIC: the background color must MATCH the pink of the peptide vials' caps and accent text shown in REFERENCES 1, 2, and 3 — but render it slightly DEEPER and more saturated than a literal pull (think rich mid-tone hot-pink/magenta-leaning pink, not too bright, not too pale). The base should look like the cap pink stepped down ~10–15% in lightness — fuller, richer, with more visual weight (NOT bubblegum, NOT salmon, NOT pastel, NOT washed out). A smooth gradient of that deeper pink (slightly brighter at the center, deeper at the edges) — clean and luminous. The overall background and the vial caps should read as the SAME family of pink but with the background sitting a touch deeper so the caps pop subtly against it. Behind the vials, integrated softly into the gradient: tasteful abstract endocrine/hormone imagery — translucent glowing white stylized hormone-molecule structures, branching steroid/peptide chemistry diagrams, elegant signaling-pathway curves, like a luminous hormone-network drifting across the background. The molecules should feel like elegant editorial decoration (not literal chemistry illustration) — soft glow, fine line art, KEPT WITHIN THE CENTER 60% OF THE CANVAS WIDTH with clear empty margins on the left and right. Do NOT push the molecules or any decorative motifs near the left or right edges of the frame — leave generous edge space for canvas scaling. Subtle bokeh light particles in the air to add atmospheric depth, also kept toward the center.

VIAL LIGHTING: bright soft directional studio light from upper front, glowing pink ambient bouncing through the glass and liquid, gentle specular highlights on the pink caps, soft drop shadows below each vial reinforcing the floating-mid-fall feel. The vials look photorealistic and integrated — clearly part of the bright pink scene.

CATEGORY TYPOGRAPHY (smaller and centered — must NOT extend near the edges): Title at the top of the canvas:
  • 'ENDOCRINE' set in Playfair Display, bold display weight, all caps, clean white, generous letter-spacing — hero word.
  • 'RESEARCH' set just below in thinner Playfair Display italic, smaller, refined editorial pairing.
  • The title occupies AT MOST the center 50–55% of the canvas width — significant empty side margins on the left and right of the title. Do NOT scale the title to span full width. Smaller, refined, museum-poster proportions.
  • Soft glow halo behind the type so it lifts from the bright pink background.
  • Optional thin minimal hairline rule under the title.
No other text anywhere — no taglines, no subtitle, no marks beyond the vial labels.

COMPOSITION FOR 4000×9600 CANVAS: leave a substantial safe margin around the edges — title should not touch the top, vials should not touch the sides or bottom. The whole composition is centered, leaving the image scalable.

Final output: photorealistic, sharp focus on the labels, vivid bright pink palette with elegant hormone-molecule motifs, vials caught mid-fall at eye level, premium luxury pharma editorial vibe, 9:16 portrait, 4K, no extra text, no watermarks, no additional vials.
